TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Tsou, story: TsouNr-pear-An

S.FORM: pan to moso la eahioa ho notfʉngʉ emʉm'ʉ to lea 'o'oanʉ.
S.TRANSL: 有一個人在工作，專注於種植吃的東西。
There existed one that was working and specialized in the planting of foods.

S.FORM: moso uh to lasi ezoeʉ 'o mameoi ho toa to isi mʉ'a ci la ana ci macucuma.
S.TRANSL: 老人去他的田裡採他種的可食的作物。
That old man went to his field and picked the crops/eaten stuff that he planted.

S.FORM: mi cu aomomane panto mo esmi ci mo eakako' to eangi.
S.TRANSL: 不久，來了一個牽著羊的人。
After a while, there came one that pulled a goat.

S.FORM: mi cu eupepeahngʉeʉ eainca aveoveoyʉ ho mo na'no euho'a 'e eh macucumasu.
S.TRANSL: (他)跟（採果的人）打招呼說，真棒，你的作物長的真好。
And he exchanged greetings by saying, “Great! Your crops are well-fruited.

S.FORM: aomane mi cu eon to jidens 'o okosi ho meecunu.
S.TRANSL: 不久，他的小孩在自行車上趕上（他）。
It was after a while and his child (=the boy) on the bike caught up with him.

S.FORM: mi cu cono skaeu 'o isi sia to jidensasi ho maine'e.
S.TRANSL: 他放一籃水果在他自行車上，回去了。
And then it was a basket (of the fruit) that he loaded onto his bike and he went home.

S.FORM: eupteʉlʉ to ceonʉ 'o oko no mamespingi ci mo mae maezo eon to jidensa.
S.TRANSL: 在路上（他）遇到一個也是騎腳踏車的女孩。
Then on the way, a girl who was also riding a bike was encountered.

S.FORM: mo apemio mi'pou 'e oko no hahocngʉ.
S.TRANSL: (因為)那男孩太專注臉朝(女孩那)邊。
(Just because) the boy was devoted to side-looking (the girl).

S.FORM: mo smotokʉ to fatu 'o isi 'o jidensasi.
S.TRANSL: 他的自行車碰到一塊石頭。
His bike bumped into a stone.

S.FORM: cu alʉ eain e'pueu ho mat'at'ango maezo.
S.TRANSL: 他跌了個四腳朝天。
And he fell on all four legs in the ground.

S.FORM: miuhu 'o isi hafa ci ci 'o'oanʉ.
S.TRANSL: 那些可以吃的東西都散開了。
And the eating stuff spilled.

S.FORM: mo panto mo esmi ci mo nanat'ot'ohaisa ci mo tueu ihe cu eaeza to'usni ho patmʉta 'o mo supepeohʉ ci la oanʉ.
S.TRANSL: 三個男孩走過來幫（他）撿起散開的水果。
There arrived three brothers that helped him picked up the spilt fruits.

S.FORM: ho soip soo'veia to jidensasi.
S.TRANSL: 把(水果)放回他的自行車。
And put them back to his bike.

S.FORM: mo 'so smo'co'congo 'e 'e oko mi cu c'o poe'oha 'o jidensasi.
S.TRANSL: 男孩覺得很痛，只能推著他著自行車。
The child felt painful and could just push his bike.

S.FORM: mi cu mo'unu maita'e 'o mo to'usnu ci mo tueu.
S.TRANSL: 那三個男孩就這樣朝那邊走了。
The three went just like that.

S.FORM: ihe aiti 'o ceopngusi mo supeohʉ.
S.TRANSL: 他們看到他的帽子掉下來。
They saw his hat fell.

S.FORM: mi cu meobango 'o mo cihi ho eoveineni.
S.TRANSL: 其中一個男孩去追（那個男孩）並且還（帽子）給那個男孩
One of the three chased (the boy) and returned it to the boy.

S.FORM: mo na'no kokaekaebʉ 'e oko ci moso smopcuku.
S.TRANSL: 之前跌倒的男孩非常高興。
The child who fell previously was then very happy.

S.FORM: isi cu fii to isi toveuca ci mo tueu ci eh la 'o'oanʉ.
S.TRANSL: 他給（那三個小孩）三個食物(他們採的)。
(The three boys) were given three foods (that were picked.)

S.FORM: mi cu mo'unu maita'e 'o mo tueu ci 'o'oko.
S.TRANSL: 那三個小孩就那樣走了。
The three went away just like that.

S.FORM: isi ouhuhuca 'o isi a faeni ci mo tueu ci mo macoconi ci la 'o'oanʉ.
S.TRANSL: 他們離開時，一面吃著那食物。
while they were walking away, they were eating the food.

S.FORM: mi cu eu'sipeohʉ ho aiti isi eainca no koesi mais'a no moh cu la eus eusno skaeu o'u toveuca.
S.TRANSL: 那老人想, 我採的不是有兩籃嗎？
(The old man thought) "Didn't it make two that I gathered?"

S.FORM: manci mi cu c'o anou cono skaeu 'e mo eon tan'e.
S.TRANSL: 怎麼只剩下一籃?
How come it remains just one basket?

S.FORM: ho uk'a eaeo si mo coni.
S.TRANSL: 另一個是空的。
And the other one was empty.

S.FORM: isi cu aoka eu'i'ia 'o mo esmi ci mo tueu ci mo oko no hahocngʉ.
S.TRANSL: 而且他仔細看那三個經過的男孩。
And he looked carefully at the three boys that arrived/passed.

S.FORM: mo 'so acʉhʉ tiatatviei to XiheX ouhuhuca.
S.TRANSL: （那三個男孩）手上都拿著...邊走邊吃。
(The three boys) all hold.... (and) ate while walking.

S.FORM: mo mo maica 'e 'a'ausnusi.
S.TRANSL: 故事就是這樣。
It's the way things are.
